title: Security Alert: Usage of Non-Standard Cryptographic Algorithms
description: Non-standard cryptographic algorithms are potentially dangerous as they can be broken by a determined attacker, leading to data compromise. It is recommended to use standard algorithms such as SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512, etc. This rule identifies instances where subclasses of java.security.MessageDigest are created.
recommendation: Replace the non-standard cryptographic algorithm with a standard one, such as SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512, etc.
impact: Using a non-standard cryptographic algorithm can lead to data breaches and security vulnerabilities. Switching to a standard algorithm enhances the security of the data.
